cards.front: What does \forall (for all) mean in the context of quantifiers?
cards.back: it means that a statement is true for every element in the domain

cards.front: What does \exists (there exists) mean in the context of quantifiers?
cards.back: it means that a statement is true for at least one element in the domain

cards.front: What type of statement is \forall used to express?
cards.back: universal statements or statements that apply to all elements in the domain

cards.front: What type of statement is \exists used to express?
cards.back: existential statements or statements that apply to at least one element in the domain

cards.front: What is the difference between \forall x P(x) and \forall y P(y)?
cards.back: \forall x P(x) means a statement is true for all elements in the domain with the variable x, while \forall y P(y) means a statement is true for all elements in the domain with the variable y
